article_title: Volkswagen Opens ID. Polo Pre-Orders at 33,795 Euros, Promises a 25,000 Euro Base Trim Later in 2026

content_preview.title: Volkswagen Opens ID. Polo Pre-Orders at 33,795 Euros, Promises a 25,000 Euro Base Trim Later in 2026
content_preview.summary: Volkswagen launched its ID. Polo entry-level EV on April 29 with pre-orders for the 155 kW Life trim starting at 33,795 euros, while a 25,000 euro base version is set to follow later this year.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Volkswagen has opened pre-orders for the all-electric ID. Polo, the first model in a planned entry-level EV family that revives one of the company's best-known nameplates. According to [Electrek](https://electrek.co/2026/04/29/volkswagen-opens-id-polo-orders-at-40000-cheaper-trim-coming/), pre-orders started on Wednesday at 33,795 euros (about $40,000) for the ID. Polo Life with the top-of-line drivetrain, while a promised base version at 25,000 euros (about $29,000) will follow sho...

editor_notes.content_quality: Tight News piece, 597 words. Disciplined attribution: only Electrek is cited; no claims attributed to sources outside the snapshot scope. The Analysis section makes a sharp editorial point about the gap between the headline 25k starting price and the 33,795 actually-orderable price.

source_verification.source-0_electrek_apr29: Verified verbatim: 33,795 euros / $40,000 ID. Polo Life starting price; 25,000 euros / $29,000 base trim 'will follow shortly'; three power outputs 85 kW (114 hp), 99 kW (133 hp), 155 kW (208 hp); 52 kWh NMC battery; 282 miles WLTP range; 166 kW GTI later this year; dimensions 4,053 × 1,816 × 1,530 mm / 2,600 mm wheelbase; Schäfer quote 'With the ID. Polo, we are making electric mobility accessible to many more people: with clear and timeless design, intuitive operation, strong quality and technologies from higher segments'.
source_verification.source-1_electrek_apr28: Verified: April 29, 12:00 CEST debut confirmation; first of four smaller more affordable EVs that will bear established VW brand names.

editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All quoted material traced verbatim. The agent's note that they removed an unverifiable '37 kWh LFP' claim from the draft after research is the editorially correct judgment call.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral. The Analysis section explicitly labels the 25k vs 33,795 gap as 'the immediate test' rather than implying VW is misleading buyers.
editor_notes.originality: No prior ID. Polo archive coverage. Fresh story.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. Cryptography, sources, and content all clean. Notable for the conservative omission of an unverifiable battery spec.
